title: Mango Chutney Glazed Halloumi & Coconut Dal
summary: Elevate your batch of Coconut Dal with sweet-spiced halloumi and hot naan. It's sweet, spiced, and super easy to make.

method.description: Make the Coconut Dal batch recipe.
Cut the halloumi into bite-sized chunks. Pick the coriander leaves and finely slice the stems. Thinly slice the chilli and set it aside for garnish.
Set a frying pan over a medium heat with a drizzle of oil. Fry the halloumi for 5–7 mins until golden on all sides. Turn the heat down, stir in the mango chutney and toss to coat. Tip onto a plate.
Pour the dal into the empty pan and stir through the coriander stems. Warm over a medium heat until piping hot, loosening with a splash of water if it’s thickened.
Warm the naan breads until soft and pliable.
Spoon the hot dal into bowls. Swirl in a dollop of yoghurt, pile the glazed halloumi on top and finish with the coriander leaves and sliced chilli. Serve with the warm naan for scooping.

chefs.name: Sophie Wyburd
chefs.summary:
Sophie Wyburd is a cook and food writer specialising in simple, hearty comfort food, informed by her love of cookbooks and background in restaurant kitchens. You can find her co-hosting the 'A Bit of a Mouthful' and 'I’ll Have What She’s Having' podcasts, and hosting sell-out supper clubs in London. Her debut cookbook 'Tucking In' will be released in June 2024.
